description:
## Problem

Auto-mode's cooldown auto-resume logic races with the existing loop. When a feature completes and auto-mode enters cooldown, the resume timer fires before the previous instance has fully cleaned up. This causes repeated `ERROR [AutoMode] Failed to auto-resume after cooldown: Auto mode is already running for main worktree` errors logged as critical severity events.

## Evidence

4 occurrences over ~4 hours (2026-03-06 21:59 - 2026-03-07 01:42), all from `AutoModeService` cooldown resume path. No work was lost but the events pollute the briefing with false critical signals.

## Root Cause

In `AutoModeService`, the cooldown resume callback calls `startAutoMode()` without first checking if the loop is still marked as running from the previous cycle. The guard `if (this.isRunning)` in `startAutoMode` rejects the call, but the error is logged at critical severity and emitted as an `auto_mode_error` event.

## Fix Required

1. In the cooldown resume callback, check `this.isRunning` before calling `startAutoMode()` — if already running, skip silently (or log at debug level)
2. Alternatively, ensure the previous loop fully cleans up `isRunning` state before the cooldown timer fires
3. Downgrade the "already running" error from critical to warn — it's an expected race, not a system failure

## Files to Investigate

- `apps/server/src/services/auto-mode-service.ts` — cooldown resume logic and `startAutoMode()` guard

## Acceptance Criteria

- No spurious `auto_mode_error` events when auto-mode naturally cycles through cooldown
- Legitimate "already running" errors (e.g., duplicate user requests) still logged appropriately
title: Bug: Auto-mode cooldown resume race condition causes spurious "already running" errors

descriptionHistory.description:
## Problem

Auto-mode's cooldown auto-resume logic races with the existing loop. When a feature completes and auto-mode enters cooldown, the resume timer fires before the previous instance has fully cleaned up. This causes repeated `ERROR [AutoMode] Failed to auto-resume after cooldown: Auto mode is already running for main worktree` errors logged as critical severity events.

## Evidence

4 occurrences over ~4 hours (2026-03-06 21:59 - 2026-03-07 01:42), all from `AutoModeService` cooldown resume path. No work was lost but the events pollute the briefing with false critical signals.

## Root Cause

In `AutoModeService`, the cooldown resume callback calls `startAutoMode()` without first checking if the loop is still marked as running from the previous cycle. The guard `if (this.isRunning)` in `startAutoMode` rejects the call, but the error is logged at critical severity and emitted as an `auto_mode_error` event.

## Fix Required

1. In the cooldown resume callback, check `this.isRunning` before calling `startAutoMode()` — if already running, skip silently (or log at debug level)
2. Alternatively, ensure the previous loop fully cleans up `isRunning` state before the cooldown timer fires

## Files to Investigate

- `apps/server/src/services/auto-mode-service.ts` — cooldown resume logic and `startAutoMode()` guard

## Acceptance Criteria

- No spurious `auto_mode_error` events when auto-mode naturally cycles through cooldown
- Legitimate "already running" errors (e.g., duplicate user requests) still logged appropriately
